name: La Vall de Laguar – Circular from Benigembla
name_original: La Vall de Laguar – Circular desde Benigembla
region_intro: Touring cycling around Benigembla, nestled in Spain's Vall de Pop, features diverse landscapes from rugged mountains to picturesque valleys. The region is characterized by summits such as El Cavall Verd and El Morro d'en Serra, alongside rolling hills covered in vineyards, almond groves, and citrus orchards. Pine forests are common on mountain climbs, and the River Xaló-Gorgos flows through the area, adding to its natural features. Remote canyons and ancient rock formations can also be found along some routes. Best touring cycling routes around Benigembla The most popular touring cycling route is Font del Llavador de Parcent – Velosol Café loop from el Campell, a 19.5 miles (31.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Vall de Laguar – Circular from Benigembla, a difficult 14.2 miles (22.9 km) path. This route provides significant elevation gain and views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Velosol Café – Xaló loop from Parcent, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail leading through the Vall de Pop, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. This difficult route includes substantial climbs and descents. Touring cycling around Benigembla is defined by rugged mountains, picturesque valleys with vineyards, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Benigembla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 20,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benigembla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Benigembla?
A: Benigembla and the surrounding Vall de Pop offer a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 70 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Benigembla?
A: The terrain around Benigembla is incredibly diverse. You'll encounter rugged mountains like El Cavall Verd and El Morro d'en Serra, offering challenging ascents and stunning vistas. The picturesque Vall de Pop features rolling hills covered in vineyards, almond groves, and citrus orchards. Pine forests are common on mountain climbs, and some routes even traverse remote canyons and ancient rock formations.

Q: Are there routes suitable for advanced touring cyclists in Benigembla?
A: Yes, Benigembla is a haven for advanced touring cyclists. Over 40 routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and challenging climbs. Notable climbs include the legendary Coll de Rates, known for its steady 5% gradient, and the ascent to Vall d'Ebo. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Velosol Café – Xaló loop from Parcent&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24725860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xaló – View of Peñón de Ifach loop from Parcent&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial climbs and descents, with some sections in Pla de Petracos reaching up to 16%.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Benigembla?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Llavador de Parcent – Velosol Café loop from el Campell&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952620041/la-vall-de-laguar-circular-from-benigembla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vall de Laguar – Circular from Benigembla&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes provide varied scenery and a convenient way to explore the area.

Q: What are some natural landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Benigembla's cycling routes offer access to numerous natural landmarks and breathtaking viewpoints. You can cycle past the scenic River Xaló-Gorgos, a site of special community interest. Higher elevations provide magnificent views of surrounding ranges like El Cavall Verd and Aitana. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2916765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Cocoll&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4757211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morro Blau - Serra de l'Aixorta&lt;/a&gt; summit.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural insights alongside its natural beauty. You might encounter traditional 18th-century 'Riuraus' – buildings used for drying grapes – scattered throughout the landscape. While cycling, you could also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castell de Guadalest and the Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site with stunning views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/276237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Skulls&lt;/a&gt;, a fascinating natural cave.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Benigembla?
A: The touring cycling experience around Benigembla is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain climbs to serene valley rides through vineyards and orchards. The quiet, well-maintained roads and breathtaking views of the Mediterranean coastline are also often highlighted as major draws.

Q: Is Benigembla a good destination for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: While many routes in Benigembla feature significant elevation, there are options suitable for families looking for a more relaxed ride. The Vall de Pop offers picturesque valley routes with rolling hills, which can be less demanding than the mountain ascents. Look for routes classified as 'easy' or 'moderate' to find suitable options for a family outing, allowing you to enjoy the vineyards and orchards at a comfortable pace.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benigembla?
A: Benigembla is an excellent destination for touring cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides and tackling the more challenging climbs. During these seasons, the vineyards and orchards also present beautiful changing colors. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay for cyclists in Benigembla and the surrounding area?
A: Yes, Benigembla and the wider Vall de Pop region are well-equipped to welcome cyclists. You'll find various cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the towns and villages along the routes, perfect for a mid-ride break or a post-cycle meal. Accommodation options range from hotels and rural houses to camping areas, such as the one near Benigembla town, catering to different preferences and budgets.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Benigembla?
A: Yes, despite the mountainous terrain, Benigembla does offer easier touring cycling options. There are 13 routes classified as 'easy' within the region. These routes typically explore the picturesque valleys, vineyards, and orchards, providing a more gentle cycling experience with less demanding climbs, perfect for a leisurely ride or those new to touring cycling.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: While Benigembla itself is not directly on a large lake, some touring cycling routes in the broader area can lead you to beautiful water features. For instance, you can find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and a refreshing change of scenery. The reservoir area also includes highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4459868" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Dam&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints.
